summary: All 5 features implemented and verified. Backend: 10/10 tests pass. (1) Login shows 'Username or Email' label+placeholder. (2) Login with email works. (3) Login with username works after setting username. (4) GET/PUT /api/auth/username endpoints working. (5) GET /api/auth/me includes 'username' field. (6) PUT /api/beneficiaries/{id} returns 'email_changed: true' when email changed and resets invitation_status to 'pending'. (7) FamilyTree legend has only one 'Blue = ...' line. Settings page Username section present for benefactor/beneficiary users (hidden for admin/operator by design).
skipped_tests: TestBeneficiaryEmailChange::test_email_change_returns_flag - Skipped (admin user has no estate, tested manually)
TestBeneficiaryEmailChange::test_no_email_change_no_flag - Skipped (admin user has no estate, tested manually)

beneficiary_email_change.details: Tested via Python script: PUT /api/beneficiaries/{id} with new email returns email_changed: true and resets invitation_status to 'pending'. Also generates new invitation_token.

login_username_or_email_label.details: LoginPage.js line 451 has label 'Username or Email', line 454 has placeholder 'Username or Email'. Native version line 263 also has placeholder.

login_with_email.details: POST /api/auth/login with email 'info@carryon.us' returns token (or OTP flow)

login_with_username.details: After setting username via PUT /api/auth/username, login with that username works. Backend line 149 falls back to username_lower lookup when email not found.

put_username_endpoint.details: PUT /api/auth/username with {username: string} sets username, enforces uniqueness (400 if taken)

auth_me_includes_username.details: GET /api/auth/me response includes 'username' field at line 958 of auth.py

settings_page_username_section.details: SettingsPage.js has Username section in Profile card (lines 308-366) with data-testid attributes for input, edit, and save buttons. Only visible for benefactor/beneficiary users (not admin/operator by design).

beneficiary_email_change_detection.details: beneficiaries.py lines 497-522: Detects email change, resets invitation_status to 'pending', generates new token, unlinks user_id, and returns email_changed: true in response.

family_tree_legend_single_line.details: FamilyTree.js lines 201-205 has only one legend line: 'Blue = estates where you're a beneficiary'. No duplicate 'Blue nodes = ...' in BeneficiariesPage.js.

beneficiaries_page_resend_prompt.details: BeneficiariesPage.js lines 255-269: When response has email_changed: true, prompts window.confirm to resend invite to new email.

critical_code_review_comments: auth.py login (line 149): Username lookup case-insensitive via username_lower field
auth.py /auth/username GET/PUT: Endpoints at lines 1274-1309, enforces uniqueness
auth.py register (line 359): New users get username=email, username_lower=email.lower()
beneficiaries.py line 909: accept_invitation sets username=email for new beneficiary users
beneficiaries.py line 551: Returns email_changed: true when email is updated
SettingsPage.js: Username section has edit/save UI with proper data-testid attributes
LoginPage.js: Both web and native versions show 'Username or Email' placeholder
FamilyTree.js: Single legend line for blue nodes (no duplicate)

success_rate.backend: 100% (10/10 tests passed, 2 skipped due to admin role)

seed_data_creation: Created and cleaned up test beneficiary for email change test
context_for_next_testing_agent: Username feature fully working. Users can set unique username in Settings, login with username or email. Beneficiary email change triggers invitation reset flow with email_changed: true flag. FamilyTree legend has single 'Blue = ...' line. Settings page Profile card (with Username section) only visible for non-staff users (benefactor/beneficiary).
